paper_use_note: Brazilian regulators/prosecutors ordered xAI/X to prevent Grok generating non-consensual sexualised deepfakes, under threat of daily fines.

title: EU — European Commission opens DSA proceedings against X over Grok deepfakes
primary_source_id: European Commission (DSA) | Proceedings opened 26 Jan 2026 (IP/26/203)
paper_use_note: The European Commission opened formal DSA proceedings against X over Grok-generated non-consensual intimate imagery and CSAM.

title: Netherlands — Amsterdam District Court orders X/xAI to stop Grok sexual-abuse content
primary_source_id: Amsterdam District Court | Order 26 Mar 2026; EUR 100,000/day penalty
paper_use_note: A Dutch court ordered X and xAI to stop Grok generating non-consensual sexual and child-abuse imagery, with a EUR 100,000/day penalty.
verification_gap: Civil injunction subject to possible appeal.

title: Spain (Almeria) — prosecutor investigates AI deepfake nudes of students
primary_source_id: Almeria Provincial Prosecutor's Office | Investigation 2025; >=18 students targeted (ClothOff)
paper_use_note: A Spanish prosecutor opened an investigation into AI-generated deepfake nude images of at least 18 schoolchildren.
verification_gap: Investigation stage. | VERIFIED: The Almeria Fiscalia investigation into 18+ minors via ClothOff was disclosed in March 2026 (revealed 17 Mar 2026 at University of Almeria), not 2025; the listed 2025 source URL is actually the Valencia case.

title: Spain (Valencia) — 17-year-old investigated over AI deepfake nudes of classmates
primary_source_id: Spanish National Police / juvenile prosecution | July 2025; 16 classmates targeted
paper_use_note: Spanish authorities are investigating a teenager for creating and selling AI deepfake nude images of 16 classmates.
verification_gap: Investigation stage; minor. | VERIFIED: The investigating authority is the Guardia Civil (case before a Valencia youth court), not the Spanish National Police.

title: SEC charges Morocoin/Berge/Cirkor + four AI 'investment clubs' in $14M crypto scam
ai_system_type: purported AI crypto-investment tips / fake AI platforms
paper_use_note: SEC alleges defendants lured investors with supposedly AI-generated tips and fake AI crypto platforms, misappropriating at least $14M.

title: DOJ indicts former iLearningEngines CEO & CFO over fabricated AI-automation revenue
primary_source_id: DOJ (EDNY) | 10-count indictment unsealed 17 Apr 2026
paper_use_note: DOJ alleges the executives inflated revenue by hundreds of millions via sham contracts while pitching iLearning as an AI automation company.

title: FTC final order vs accessiBe over deceptive AI web-accessibility claims
primary_source_id: FTC | Matter 2223156; final order Apr 2025; $1,000,000
paper_use_note: FTC found accessiBe falsely claimed its AI tool could make any website WCAG-compliant and used undisclosed paid reviews; $1M order.

title: FTC final order vs Sitejabber (Govest) over AI review-platform misrepresentations
primary_source_id: FTC | Complaint Nov 2024; final consent order Jan 2025
paper_use_note: FTC found Sitejabber deceptively presented pre-purchase ratings as genuine post-experience reviews, inflating ratings/review counts.
verification_gap: Consent order without admission. | VERIFIED: The FTC respondent is GGL Projects, Inc. (d/b/a Sitejabber), not 'Govest'.

title: FTC settlement vs Cox Media Group over fake 'Active Listening' AI ad-targeting
primary_source_id: FTC | Press release 21 May 2026; ~$930,000
paper_use_note: FTC charged firms marketed an AI 'active listening' service that captured device voice for ad targeting, when it actually resold data-broker lists.
verification_gap: Proposed settlement, not yet final; no admission.

title: Massachusetts AG settlement vs Earnest over AI student-loan underwriting fair-lending
primary_source_id: Massachusetts AG | 10 July 2025; $2.5M Assurance of Discontinuance
paper_use_note: The Massachusetts AG settled allegations that Earnest failed to mitigate disparate-impact fair-lending risk in its AI underwriting models ($2.5M AOD).

title: Pennsylvania AG settlement vs Home365 over AI property-management failures
primary_source_id: Pennsylvania AG | AVC, Allegheny County; announced June 2025; $45,000
paper_use_note: The Pennsylvania AG settled allegations that Home365's AI-based platform contributed to maintenance delays and unsafe housing ($45K AVC).
